SEARCH
Package DAO

Class EmployeeDAO

java.lang.Object
DAO.EmployeeDAO
public class EmployeeDAO extends Object
Data Access Object (DAO) for managing Employee data. Handles authentication, creation, updates, and deletion of employee records, as well as subscriber creation which is an employee-privileged action.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    EmployeeDAO()

  • Constructor Details

    • EmployeeDAO

      public EmployeeDAO()

  • Method Details

    • login

      public Employee login(String userName, String password) throws SQLException
      Authenticates an employee against the database. Verifies username and password, and if successful, returns a fully populated Employee object.
      Parameters:
      userName - The username provided during login.
      password - The password provided during login.
      Returns:
      An Employee object if credentials are valid, null otherwise.
      Throws:
      SQLException - If a database error occurs.

    • checkIfUsernameIsAlreadyTaken

      public Employee checkIfUsernameIsAlreadyTaken(String userName) throws SQLException
      Checks if a given username already exists in the database. Used during the registration process to prevent duplicate usernames.
      Parameters:
      userName - The username to check.
      Returns:
      An Employee object if the username is taken, null otherwise.
      Throws:
      SQLException - If a database error occurs.

    • createSubscriber

      public boolean createSubscriber(Customer customer)
      Creates a new subscriber (Customer) in the database. Generates a new customer ID and updates the passed Customer object.
      Parameters:
      customer - The Customer object containing subscriber details.
      Returns:
      true if the subscriber was successfully created.

    • createEmployee

      public boolean createEmployee(Employee emp) throws SQLException
      Inserts a new employee record into the database. Retrieves the generated employee ID and updates the Employee object.
      Parameters:
      emp - The Employee object to create.
      Returns:
      true if the employee was successfully added.
      Throws:
      SQLException - If a database error occurs.

    • updateEmployeeDetails

      public boolean updateEmployeeDetails(Employee employee)
      Updates an existing employee's details (password, phone, email). The update is performed based on the username.
      Parameters:
      employee - The Employee object with updated details.
      Returns:
      true if the update was successful.

    • deleteEmployee

      public boolean deleteEmployee(int id) throws SQLException
      Removes an employee record from the database by ID.
      Parameters:
      id - The ID of the employee to delete.
      Returns:
      true if the deletion was successful.
      Throws:
      SQLException - If a database error occurs.
